recentTraces.summary: Turned Command Cockpit Event Pulse packets into selectable recent-event sockets with an in-board Event Lens. The lens stays inside the Quest level map, showing event type, title, and time from reusable trail records while preserving the same cockpit footprint.

Added a procedural Command Cockpit Event Pulse texture plus data-bound recent-event packets inside the Quest level map. Trail records, blockers, proof, and unlocks now animate through the existing board footprint, making the selected path feel active without increasing cockpit height.

Added a generated Command Cockpit Node Halos texture plus data-derived medals inside each Quest level-map cell. Nodes now show compact proof, gate, trail, crew, score, and next-move state from reusable lane counts and checkpoint data, making the board denser without increasing page height.

Added a generated Command Cockpit Crew Relay texture plus a compact bot handoff chip in the Quest focus lens. Selected path nodes now surface avatar, callsign, readiness, gated/staged tone, and a COM jump from reusable Agent Party data, making bot ownership visible without increasing scroll.

Added a generated Command Cockpit Focus Beam texture plus a computed in-board reticle layer. World, Level, Checkpoint, Gate, and Next clicks now move a tone-aware beam across the Quest level map, making cockpit motion feel tied to selected path state instead of decorative page chrome.

Added a generated Command Cockpit Insight Ribbon texture plus a focused-node HUD overlay inside the Quest level map. World, Level, Checkpoint, Gate, and Next clicks now project compact milestone, blocker, bot, proof, and next-action signals from reusable lane, trail, gate, asset, and agent data.

Added a generated Lane Expansion Portal Deck texture plus data-driven Worlds Launch socket buttons, turning future lane capacity into compact minigame portals that reuse existing Loom, Arcade, Registry, Genesis, and Asset routes without adding another long-scroll section.

Added a project-local Agent Constellation Bay bitmap plus data-driven live agent beacons to the Agent Sprite Foundry, making bot identities feel more like selectable game characters while preserving reusable lane and agent data contracts.

Added an animated role-aware echo rail to the Command Cockpit Quest board, deriving realm, level, checkpoint, gate, and next-action signals from reusable lane data so clicked paths expose more history without adding scroll.

Added role-aware signal packets to the Command Cockpit Quest focus lens, deriving world, level, checkpoint, gate, and next-action telemetry from reusable lane data so selected nodes feel deeper inside the existing board.

Turned the Command Cockpit Quest level map sockets into selectable board nodes with a compact focus lens. World, Level, Checkpoint, Gate, and Next can now reveal in-board detail for every lane, making the selected path feel expandable without adding another report row.

Added cockpit-aware detail-panel settling plus a tighter Stable Board visual pass so Command Cockpit lane changes update in place, the overview HUD compresses further, and the Quest board carries calmer premium scan motion without adding another vertical section.

Added a compact path-depth stack inside the Command Cockpit Quest level map, deriving latest event, level depth, gate pressure, and next move from lane data so future lanes inherit expandable-feeling strata inside the existing playable surface.

Added a compact animated minigame sigil to the Command Cockpit current-run hero, deriving initials and motion family from lane minigame metadata so future lanes get a distinct playable identity without creating another scroll row.
Added GATE/WIN/RUN/XP signal glyphs, badge state metadata, and a compact sweep animation to the Command Cockpit Overview signal strip without adding another row or increasing scroll pressure.

Converted the Command Cockpit mounted-stage footer rail from sticky layout content into a compact absolute glass HUD overlay with smaller status cells, preserving status at a glance while reducing vertical document pressure.
Removed the auto-placed cockpit stage-card grid from Overview, tightened signal/action strips into HUD-sized controls, and preserved the Quest level map as the first-screen route state.
Added role metadata, current-state flags, map/XP/active/lock/go glyphs, and active socket pulsing to the Command Cockpit Quest level map while keeping the narrow mobile route flattened and readable.

Turned the Command Cockpit Overview Quest field into a compact in-stage level map with route bed, node sockets, progress runner, and desktop stagger while preserving the same World, Level, Checkpoint, Gate, and Next data cells.

Turned the Command Cockpit lane rail into a two-column territory board with active-lane index metadata, a route line, animated runner, and per-lane route nodes while keeping the narrow mobile lane picker compact.

Added active stage index metadata, an animated compass runner, and module sockets to the Command Cockpit Overview dock, turning Quest, Runway, Crew, Forge, Gates, and Intel into a compact level-selector surface without adding scroll height.

Generated and integrated a reusable Command Cockpit Mission Cartridge texture, applied it behind the selected-lane status HUD, tightened mobile HUD rows and map strip height, and registered the texture in the Visual Asset Vault as standalone system art.

Turned each selected-lane owner portrait into an animated character socket driven by existing callsign, specialty, accent, and sprite-sheet cell data, improving bot identity inside the Command Cockpit HUD without adding scroll height or schema fields.

Added a compact four-cell event ribbon inside the Command Cockpit Overview stage for latest event, proof, gate, and next move, with a sweep runner and mobile containment so the selected lane shows recent state without another report stack.

Added a six-node World, Level, Quest, Proof, Gate, and Bot strip inside the Command Cockpit Overview HUD, with a progress-driven runner and mobile horizontal containment so the selected lane reads like a mission map before deeper scrolling.

Added a lane-colored parallax depth-ring layer inside the bounded Command Cockpit Overview stage, tied ring rotation to lane progress, and tuned mobile opacity while suppressing the heavier phone holo floor so the animation adds premium arena motion without covering the mission board.

Compressed the Command Cockpit lane cards into shorter cartridges with smaller avatars, one-line labels, tiny level/gate/win/live cells, and retained signal pips so more money paths are visible before rail scrolling.

Collapsed the Cockpit Overview bot handoff into a compact crew chip, trimmed its copy density, and added a soft right-edge focus fade so the mounted stage stops feeling like several crowded report panels.

Projected a lane-state holo floor behind the compact Cockpit Overview mission module with progress, quest, proof, gates, and trail pads, plus runner/sweep animation, without adding scroll or new report cards.

Added a quest field board with world, level, checkpoint, gate, and next-move cells on an animated route rail, then used it to replace the taller next/gate report row inside the Command Cockpit Overview.

Scoped the Command Cockpit Overview HUD to hide duplicate bot event copy and the nested command matrix, keeping lane identity, stats, and route pulse while giving the mounted stage more first-screen authority.
Pinned the mounted-stage footer rail inside the desktop Command Cockpit Overview stage so level, quest, gate, and next-move cells remain readable instead of falling below the scrollable stage.

Added a mounted-stage footer rail with level, quest progress, gate pressure, and next move; anchored it to the overview stage bottom, added a sweep animation, and pulled it above the mobile bottom dock so the reclaimed floor reads as useful game HUD instead of dead space.

Scoped the narrow Command Cockpit Quest module to a smaller objective hero, hidden long run copy, compact signal cells, tighter action buttons, and an animated scan line so the phone stage reads like a game objective instead of a report card.

Scoped the Overview dock, meter, module buttons, and Overview detail-section spacing to a denser Command Cockpit treatment so the first selected-lane module reads like a compact mission board.

Scoped the sticky stage lens to a thinner Cockpit strip with tighter columns, hidden meta copy, and denser signal cells while preserving the full lens elsewhere.

Compressed the Map stage playback and lane-focus overlays into compact HUD strips, hiding long copy and preserving selected path stats without covering the playable atlas board.

Added a sticky stage lens inside the mounted lane stage so the active view, level XP, gate pressure, active work, and trail depth stay visible while scrolling inside the cockpit.
Compacted the selected-lane HUD controls into one bounded command matrix, grouping the micro chronicle, view portals, transition meter, and lane switch chips into a tighter game-control deck.

Added a compact level-select switch deck inside the active lane HUD so the current lane and highest-pressure paths can be reached without relying on the long lane rail.

globalGates: No legal/KYC/tax/billing/account-contract commitments without explicit user confirmation.
No real-money trades, deposits, withdrawals, or seed/private-key storage by autonomous agents.
No public claims/comments/submissions unless lane owner and route are explicitly assigned.
Every lane must record source, hypothesis, proof artifact, blocker, risk, and next action.

items.title: Command Cockpit Event Lens
items.summary: Let recent-event packets explain what happened inside the Quest map without adding scroll.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Turned Command Cockpit Event Pulse packets into selectable recent-event sockets with an in-board Event Lens. The lens stays inside the Quest level map, showing event type, title, and time from reusable trail records while preserving the same cockpit footprint.

items.title: Command Cockpit Event Pulse
items.summary: Make recent lane events move through the Quest map without adding another scroll row.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added a procedural Command Cockpit Event Pulse texture plus data-bound recent-event packets inside the Quest level map. Trail records, blockers, proof, and unlocks now animate through the existing board footprint, making the selected path feel active without increasing cockpit height.

items.title: Command Cockpit Node Halos
items.summary: Make Quest nodes show proof, blockers, crew, score, trail, and next-move medals without adding scroll.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added a generated Command Cockpit Node Halos texture plus data-derived medals inside each Quest level-map cell. Nodes now show compact proof, gate, trail, crew, score, and next-move state from reusable lane counts and checkpoint data, making the board denser without increasing page height.

items.title: Command Cockpit Crew Relay
items.summary: Show the responsible bot inside the clicked Quest node lens without adding a crew panel.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added a generated Command Cockpit Crew Relay texture plus a compact bot handoff chip in the Quest focus lens. Selected path nodes now surface avatar, callsign, readiness, gated/staged tone, and a COM jump from reusable Agent Party data, making bot ownership visible without increasing scroll.

items.title: Command Cockpit Focus Beam
items.summary: Make clicked Quest nodes drive a moving focus reticle without adding scroll.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added a generated Command Cockpit Focus Beam texture plus a computed in-board reticle layer. World, Level, Checkpoint, Gate, and Next clicks now move a tone-aware beam across the Quest level map, making cockpit motion feel tied to selected path state instead of decorative page chrome.

items.title: Command Cockpit Insight Ribbon
items.summary: Make clicked Quest nodes reveal milestone, blocker, bot, proof, and next-move signals without adding scroll.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added a generated Command Cockpit Insight Ribbon texture plus a focused-node HUD overlay inside the Quest level map. World, Level, Checkpoint, Gate, and Next clicks now project compact milestone, blocker, bot, proof, and next-action signals from reusable lane, trail, gate, asset, and agent data.

items.title: Lane Expansion Portal Deck
items.summary: Make future lanes and lane-specific minigames visible as compact unlock sockets.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added a generated Lane Expansion Portal Deck texture plus data-driven Worlds Launch socket buttons, turning future lane capacity into compact minigame portals that reuse existing Loom, Arcade, Registry, Genesis, and Asset routes without adding another long-scroll section.

items.title: Agent Constellation Bay
items.summary: Give the bot identity surface a generated constellation texture with live agent beacons.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added a project-local Agent Constellation Bay bitmap plus data-driven live agent beacons to the Agent Sprite Foundry, making bot identities feel more like selectable game characters while preserving reusable lane and agent data contracts.

items.title: Command Cockpit Node Echo Rail

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added an animated role-aware echo rail to the Command Cockpit Quest board, deriving realm, level, checkpoint, gate, and next-action signals from reusable lane data so clicked paths expose more history without adding scroll.

items.title: Command Cockpit Node Signal Packets

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Added role-aware signal packets to the Command Cockpit Quest focus lens, deriving world, level, checkpoint, gate, and next-action telemetry from reusable lane data so selected nodes feel deeper inside the existing board.

items.title: Implementation
items.summary: Turned the Command Cockpit Quest level map sockets into selectable board nodes with a compact focus lens. World, Level, Checkpoint, Gate, and Next can now reveal in-board detail for every lane, making the selected path feel expandable without adding another report row.
